content.text: Once a default output folder is chosen, the button label doesn't make it obvious that it is about a filesystem folder. Does it make sense to show an eclipsed (...) version of the full path here?[Image #1]

addedLines: - claude: Catch-all for any task that doesn't fit a more specific agent. FleetView's default when no agent name is typed. (Tools: *)

- claude-code-guide: Use this agent when the user asks questions ("Can Claude...", "Does Claude...", "How do I...") about: (1) Claude Code (the CLI tool) - features, hooks, slash commands, MCP servers, settings, IDE integrations, keyboard shortcuts; (2) Claude Agent SDK - building custom agents; (3) Claude API (formerly Anthropic API) - API usage, tool use, Anthropic SDK usage. **IMPORTANT:** Before spawning a new agent, check if there is already a running or recently completed claude-code-guide agent that you can continue via SendMessage. (Tools: Bash, Read, WebFetch, WebSearch)

- entire-search: Search Entire checkpoint history and transcripts with `entire search --json`. Use proactively when the user asks about previous work, commits, sessions, prompts, or historical context in this repository. (Tools: Bash)

- Explore: Fast read-only search agent for locating code. Use it to find files by pattern (eg. "src/components/**/*.tsx"), grep for symbols or keywords (eg. "API endpoints"), or answer "where is X defined / which files reference Y." Do NOT use it for code review, design-doc auditing, cross-file consistency checks, or open-ended analysis — it reads excerpts rather than whole files and will miss content past its read window. When calling, specify search breadth: "quick" for a single targeted lookup, "medium" for moderate exploration, or "very thorough" to search across multiple locations and naming conventions. (Tools: All tools except Agent, ExitPlanMode, Edit, Write, NotebookEdit)

- general-purpose: General-purpose agent for researching complex questions, searching for code, and executing multi-step tasks. When you are searching for a keyword or file and are not confident that you will find the right match in the first few tries use this agent to perform the search for you. (Tools: *)

- Plan: Software architect agent for designing implementation plans. Use this when you need to plan the implementation strategy for a task. Returns step-by-step plans, identifies critical files, and considers architectural trade-offs. (Tools: All tools except Agent, ExitPlanMode, Edit, Write, NotebookEdit)
- statusline-setup: Use this agent to configure the user's Claude Code status line setting. (Tools: Read, Edit)

attachment.content:
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review changed code for reuse, quality, and efficiency, then fix any issues found.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

13	function applyTemplate(template: string): { value: string; cursorPos: number } {
14	  const date = new Date().toISOString().split('T')[0]
15	  const withDate = template.replace('{date}', date)
16	  const nameIdx = withDate.indexOf('{name}')
17	  if (nameIdx === -1) return { value: withDate, cursorPos: withDate.length }
18	  return { value: withDate.replace('{name}', ''), cursorPos: nameIdx }
19	}

content.text:
Now I have the full picture. The button at `SplitMode/index.tsx:126` currently calls `basename(outputFolder)` which strips the full path down to just the folder name. The fix is to show a truncated path like `…/example-pdfs-split` instead.

I'd add a small `ellipsisPath` utility alongside `basename` in `utils.ts`, then use it in the button label. Something like:

```
/Users/ben/git/paper-scan-processor → …/paper-scan-processor
```

Should this apply to both SplitMode (output folder) and MergeMode (the merged file path at line 181), or just SplitMode for now?

message.content: Now there is quite some whitespace after the folder name. Is it possible to show as much of the path as possible, starting from the right?

content.text: The issue is that `ellipsisPath` always shows only the basename, leaving the rest of the available space empty. The right fix is to show the full path and use CSS `direction: rtl` to truncate from the left — that way it fills the space and always shows the rightmost portion.

message.content: Is it possible to align the path to the left? Also, it should start with a `/`

content.text: There's no pure CSS way to get left-aligned + left-truncated + starts-with-`/`. The right approach is to compute which trailing path segments fit using canvas measurement, then render just those.

content.text: Clean. The logic: after the DOM is laid out, canvas measures the pixel width of each trailing path segment (`/git/paper-scan-processor/example-pdfs-split`, `/paper-scan-processor/example-pdfs-split`, `/example-pdfs-split`, …) and picks the longest one that fits. The result is always left-aligned and starts with `/`. Since `useLayoutEffect` runs before the browser paints, there's no flash.

content.text: That confirms the fundamental problem — `direction: rtl` inherently anchors the text to the right, so the leading `/` is the first thing to disappear when the path overflows. The `trailingPath` approach (last 2 segments, LTR) is actually the right fix: it always starts with `/`, shows the most relevant part, and needs no special CSS.

message.content: The `/` also disappears if there is enough space to display it. Ask before changing code

content.text:
That's a Unicode BiDi rendering issue — with `direction: rtl`, the leading `/` (a neutral character) gets reordered by the bidi algorithm relative to the following LTR text, so it ends up misplaced or invisible even when there's room.

A few directions we could go:

1. **Drop `direction: rtl` entirely** — show the full path LTR, truncated on the right with `…`. Simple, predictable, always starts with `/`. The tradeoff is the folder name at the end may be cut off for long paths.

2. **`unicode-bidi: bidi-override`** — forces strict RTL character ordering. This would reverse the characters visually (`tilps-fdp-elpmaxe/...`), so not useful here.

3. **Overflow scroll trick** — render the full path LTR inside an `overflow: hidden` container and set `scrollLeft` to the end, so the rightmost content (the folder name) is what's visible. No bidi, no string manipulation. The left clip is a clean character cut but may land mid-word.

4. **`trailingPath` (last N segments)** — deterministic, always starts with `/`, shows the folder name and its parent. We've been back and forth on this one.

Which of these feels right to you?

hookInfos.command: sh -c 'if ! command -v entire >/dev/null 2>&1; then exit 0; fi; exec entire hooks claude-code stop'

message.content: Let's try 3.

content.text: I'll add a `ref` and `useLayoutEffect` to scroll the container to its rightmost position after each render. Remove `direction: rtl` and `text-overflow: ellipsis` — just `overflow: hidden` doing the clipping.

Auf Branch main Änderungen, die nicht zum Commit vorgemerkt sind: (benutzen Sie "git add ...", um die Änderungen zum Commit vorzumerken) (benutzen Sie "git restore ...", um die Änderungen im Arbeitsverzeichnis zu verwerfen) geändert: package.json geändert: package.json.md5 geändert: src/components/SplitMode/OutputFileHeader.tsx geändert: src/components/SplitMode/index.tsx geändert: src/utils.ts Unversionierte Dateien: (benutzen Sie "git add ...", um die Änderungen zum Commit vorzumerken) ../.DS_Store ../.claude/ ../.entire/ ../app_test.go ../example-pdfs-merge/ ../example-pdfs-split/ src/components/SplitMode/ClippedPath.tsx ../paper-scan-processor keine Änderungen zum Commit vorgemerkt (benutzen Sie "git add" und/oder "git commit -a") diff --git a/frontend/package.json b/frontend/package.json index cb358fd..10e438d 100644 --- a/frontend/package.json +++ b/frontend/package.json @@ -6,7 +6,8 @@ "scripts": { "dev": "vite", "build": "tsc && vite build", - "preview": "vite preview" + "preview": "vite preview", + "tsc": "tsc" }, "dependencies": { "@dnd-kit/core": "^6.3.1", diff --git a/frontend/package.json.md5 b/frontend/package.json.md5 index b16c5d4..9b0b5c5 100755 --- a/frontend/package.json.md5 +++ b/frontend/package.json.md5 @@ -1 +1 @@ -54997d8a8bf5bf4becf7fc81fa196446 \ No newline at end of file +2f135a553bbdafcee8c0f2a9a7d72ac1 \ No newline at end of file diff --git a/frontend/src/components/SplitMode/OutputFileHeader.tsx b/frontend/src/components/SplitMode/OutputFileHeader.tsx index a8b326f..d999c22 100644 --- a/frontend/src/components/SplitMode/OutputFileHeader.tsx +++ b/frontend/src/components/SplitMode/OutputFileHeader.tsx @@ -1,5 +1,6 @@ import { ITEM_PADDING, HEADER_HEIGHT } from '../../constants' import { PendingFocusHandle } from './usePendingFocus' +import ClippedPath from './ClippedPath' interface Props { filename: string @@ -56,19 +57,7 @@ export default function OutputFileHeader({ /> .pdf -
- {folder ?? 'Choose folder…'} -
+ ) } diff --git a/frontend/src/components/SplitMode/index.tsx b/frontend/src/components/SplitMode/index.tsx index 493b988..694f1fc 100644 --- a/frontend/src/components/SplitMode/index.tsx +++ b/frontend/src/components/SplitMode/index.tsx @@ -4,7 +4,7 @@ import { notifications } from '@mantine/notifications' import ThumbnailPanel from './ThumbnailPanel' import DetailPanel from '../DetailPanel' import { OpenFile, OpenPDF, PageCount, PickFolder, ExportSplit } from '../../../wailsjs/go/main/App' -import { basename } from '../../utils' +import { basename, ellipsisPath } from '../../utils' import { useOutputFiles } from './useOutputFiles' import { usePendingFocus } from './usePendingFocus' @@ -123,7 +123,7 @@ export default function SplitMode({ initialPath }: Props) { style={{ flex: 1 }} />

content.content:
8802ec2 Move usePendingFocus into separate file
d1d70f0 Extract OutputFileHeader into its own file
03bca29 Validate unique filenames before export in SplitMode
0cb0e94 Rename file containing TS types for Go interface
af19c70 Show success modal after export, with button to open outPath in file manager

input.command:
cd /Users/ben/git/paper-scan-processor && git add frontend/src/components/SplitMode/ClippedPath.tsx frontend/src/components/SplitMode/OutputFileHeader.tsx frontend/src/components/SplitMode/index.tsx frontend/src/utils.ts frontend/package.json frontend/package.json.md5 && git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
Show output folder paths with right-anchored clipping and ellipsis

Adds ClippedPath component that scrolls to the right end of a path so
the filename is always visible, overlays '…' when clipped, and shows
the full path in a tooltip. Uses scrollLeft instead of direction:rtl to
avoid Unicode BiDi issues with the leading slash.

The output folder button in SplitMode uses ellipsisPath (…/folder-name)
for a compact label.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)"
